source_intake_record.raw_question_text: In the load circuit shown, with a resistor value \( R = 50 \Omega \) and an inductive reactance \( X_{\mathrm{L}} = 50 \Omega \), when the inductor inside the dashed box is short-circuited, \( i = 2 \sin (\omega t - 15^{\circ}) \, \mathrm{A} \). When the inductor is operating normally, the instantaneous value expression for the circuit current \( i \) is

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'load circuit shown', so the circuit topology is referenced as visual information
the task depends on the inductor 'inside the dashed box', a spatial/diagram-specific reference that is not fully specified in text alone
asset_registry_record.image_manifest shows an existing image file
multi_step: the question gives one operating condition (inductor short-circuited) and asks for current under a second operating condition (inductor normal), which suggests condition-to-condition reasoning
the text provides circuit parameters R=50 ohm and X_L=50 ohm plus a phase-shifted current expression, indicating more than direct lookup
the answer is an instantaneous current expression, implying conversion or combination of magnitude and phase information rather than single-step extraction
multiple-choice options vary in both amplitude and phase, consistent with at least two reasoning dimensions
verifiability: the answer is a precise mathematical expression for current with explicit amplitude and phase
choices are provided, so the final answer can be checked against a finite candidate set
answer_text matches one of the listed options exactly
